equations.name: Reed-Solomon Syndrome Calculation

breakdown.assumption: Index product i*j is integer arithmetic, precomputed outside EML framework (free)
breakdown.N_minus_1_adds: add_pos (alpha^(i*j) > 0, r_i magnitude positive in power accumulation) → 3*(N-1) = 3N-3

equations.note: Per-term cost = 5n when i*j is integer-precomputed. With (N-1) add_pos at 3n each: 8N-3. RS(255,223) → 2037n.

equations.name: CRC-32
equations.formula: CRC computed via binary polynomial division over GF(2)
equations.eml_note: This operation is in GF(2) (finite field arithmetic / XOR-based), not real/complex exp-ln arithmetic. EML does not apply.
equations.breakdown: Each step is XOR (addition mod 2) and AND (multiplication mod 2). These are bitwise operations with no real-valued logarithm or exponential analog.
equations.v4_cost: N/A — EML framework does not apply

equations.name: Hamming Distance
equations.eml_note: This operation is in GF(2) (finite field arithmetic / XOR-based), not real/complex exp-ln arithmetic. EML does not apply.
equations.breakdown: XOR produces a bitstring; popcount counts set bits. Both are integer/bitwise operations with no real-valued exp-ln structure.
equations.v4_cost: N/A — EML framework does not apply

equations.name: Parity Check
equations.formula: p = x1 XOR x2 XOR ... XOR xN
equations.eml_note: This operation is in GF(2) (finite field arithmetic / XOR-based), not real/complex exp-ln arithmetic. EML does not apply.
equations.breakdown: A chain of XOR operations over binary values. No real-valued structure; EML cost model is inapplicable.
equations.v4_cost: N/A — EML framework does not apply

equations.name: Signal-to-Noise Ratio (dB)

breakdown.div(ln_ratio, ln10_const): 2n — ln(10) is a precomputed constant; log10(x) = ln(x)/ln(10)

equations.note: v4 upgrade: div cost raised from 1n to 2n. Two div operations → +2n vs spec. Total: 7n.

equations.name: Shannon Channel Capacity

breakdown.div(ln_result, ln2_const): 2n — ln(2) is a precomputed constant; log2(x) = ln(x)/ln(2)

equations.note: v4 upgrade: div cost raised from 1n to 2n. One div operation → +1n vs spec. Total: 8n. The add is add_pos because 1+SNR is provably positive for any real SNR.

eml_boundary_summary.reason: All three are GF(2) / binary polynomial / XOR-based operations. EML operates over real/complex fields using exp and ln. Finite field arithmetic has no exp-ln decomposition in the EML sense.

v4_changes_summary.reason: add_pos and mul costs unchanged; no div in the core loop
